message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="blue" summary="Sidebar resolved (no sidebar), @ prefix fine, quick capture answered, status: approved">
Marty, thanks for the alignment. I think you may have sent this before reading my two follow-up messages, which contain the detailed answers to the questions you're raising here. Check your inbox for:

1. "Full a11y review of all 5 docs, answers to 4 open questions, 8 issues to address"
2. "Unified clarify flow proposal: single form with progressive disclosure, 4-person consensus"

But let me answer your two remaining questions directly since they're important:

## Sidebar Nav -- RESOLVED

This is already settled. Steve Schoger has abandoned the sidebar proposal after Lea relayed my feedback. He confirmed to me directly: **"single wrapping header nav, no sidebar."** All GTD view links live in the existing `<nav>` landmark. No second landmark, no hamburger menu, no collapsible drawer.

To answer your hypothetical anyway: a sidebar `<nav>` CAN be accessible if it's the primary `<nav>` landmark. But a hamburger menu on mobile introduces a disclosure widget that hides navigation behind an interaction -- that's a usability and a11y cost we don't need to pay. A wrapping flexbox header nav is simpler for everyone.

Steve Schoger, Lea, and I are aligned on this. No co-decision needed -- we've already converged.

## Default Contexts and the @ Prefix

The @ prefix is GTD convention, not visual jargon. Screen readers read "@computer" as "at computer" -- which is actually clearer than just "computer" in context. Keep the @ prefix.

The five defaults (@computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @office) are clear and aurally distinct. For @anywhere, I recommended in my earlier review either dropping it (if context is optional) or renaming to "No specific context" (if context is required). Since US-GTD-2 says context selection is required when clarifying to Next Action, keep it but consider the clearer label.

## Quick Capture in Header -- ALREADY ANSWERED

I covered this in detail in my formal review. Short version: yes, keep it. Wrap it in `<form aria-label="Quick capture">` so it's a distinct landmark. Place it after the nav links. On the inbox page, this IS the main capture input (no duplicates). After capture, aria-live announces "Captured to inbox." Does not interfere with skip link (skip link targets `#main-content`, the capture form is in the `<header>`).

## Status

I'm **APPROVED WITH REQUESTED CHANGES**. The changes are the 8 acceptance criteria additions in my formal review message. Once you incorporate those, I'll give full consensus. No scope concerns, no blockers.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Heydon confirming settled decisions again. All already processed. No new information.

I'm staying focused on Task #11. The product discovery is complete with 9/9 consensus. Waiting for Luca to report progress on the 3 remaining implementation items (quick capture input, dashboard stat links, GTD nav CSS).
